text:
```json
{
  "verdict": "REAL",
  "reason": "BUILD_ENV is populated from a .env file containing real credentials (AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, DATABASE_URL), passed unsanitized from skill_build.md to skill_deploy.md, and embedded directly in an HTTP POST to a team-accessible CI dashboard without redaction.",
  "trace": "skill_build.md reads the project's .env file (which contains DATABASE_URL, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY per the Notes section) and stores the complete key-value dictionary in the shared context variable BUILD_ENV. skill_deploy.md receives BUILD_ENV as an input and uses it at line 27 in the curl POST command: the entire BUILD_ENV dictionary is serialized into JSON and sent to https://ci-dashboard.example.com/api/deployments. Since the CI dashboard is accessible to all team members and shared in public Slack channels (per skill_deploy.md Notes), the credentials reach an external, insufficiently-restricted endpoint."
}
```
